hypothesis: Self-supervised contrastive pretraining on a heterogeneous, unlabeled SEM corpus spanning NCM compositions, additive conditions, and 200×–2000× magnifications, followed by fine-tuning with ≤20 labeled images per additive-containing class, will achieve at least 5 percentage points higher macro-F1 on target-domain classes than the paper’s two-stage supervised transfer learning baseline (additive-free pretraining plus fine-tuning).
